steps.text: Saute aromatics: In a large pot over medium heat, warm oil and melt butter. Add onion, celery, and carrots. Cook until softened. Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds.
Make a light roux: Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and stir well. Cook 1–2 minutes to remove raw flour taste.
Add potatoes and stock: Stir in diced potatoes, then slowly add chicken stock while stirring. Add bay leaves, thyme, paprika, black pepper, and salt. Bring to a gentle boil.
Simmer until tender: Reduce heat and simmer until potatoes are very tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
Mash for texture: Remove bay leaves. Mash some of the potatoes in the pot with a spoon/potato masher to thicken, leaving some chunks.
Add dairy and smoked meat: Stir in milk and optional cream. Add chopped smoked turkey. Warm gently (avoid a hard boil).
Optional cheese finish: If using cheddar, stir in gradually until melted and smooth. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Serve: Ladle into bowls and garnish with green onions and parsley.
